steps.title: Spot the key clue about timing
steps.content: The passage states that the dodder plant increases its reproductive success by flowering "at the same time" as its host, and that it achieves this ______ by absorbing a protein the host produces just before flowering. The blank names the phenomenon of two things happening simultaneously.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
Test each choice against the passage's description of dodder and host flowering at the same time:
  • Choice A — synchronization: the coordination of events to occur at the same time — exactly describes the dodder matching its flowering to the host's bloom via the absorbed protein. ✓
  • Choice B — hibernation: a state of dormancy or inactivity — the passage describes active flowering, not dormancy. ✗
  • Choice C — prediction: the act of declaring something in advance — the dodder uses a protein signal to flower at the same time, not to forecast when flowering will happen. ✗
  • Choice D — moderation: the act of reducing intensity or extremity — nothing in the passage involves lessening or restraining anything. ✗
A is correct.
